--- NEW ADDITIONS ---
User asked about the British Museum.
Assistant provided a detailed guide to the British Museum, covering its history, location, mission, size, scope, key departments, most-famous objects, architecture, admission, opening hours, visitor tips, research & learning, and cultural impact.
User inquired about the country whose artifacts are most represented in the British Museum.
Assistant provided a breakdown of the largest single geographic groups in the British Museum's core collection, including Egypt & Sudan, Classical Antiquities, Near East, Asia, Africa & Oceania, Europe, and Americas.
User asked why certain regions dominate the British Museum's collection.
Assistant explained the historical factors that contributed to the dominance of Egypt, Greece/Rome, and the Near East in the British Museum's collection, including early-19th-century "Egyptomania," Classical antiquities as the "core of civilization," British imperial archaeology in the Near East, legal frameworks & "gift" culture, and acquisition budgets & collecting policies.
User requested a quick visual cue of the distribution of catalogued items by region.
